notes: This policy is intentionally current-tree grounded. It names the first store families that already behave like crown-jewel local state in the repo.
The goal of this first slice is to make backup and restore assumptions explicit before convenience rebuild flows silently normalize data loss.

store_families.backup_frequency_expectation: after any topic bootstrap or workspace mutation batch
store_families.restore_expectation: restore the last known-good snapshot, then run the topic workspace registry validator before any scheduler use
notes: This file is local operator state, not Git-backed truth.
If it grows to include months of local workspace routing decisions, convenience regeneration is not an acceptable recovery story.

store_families.backup_frequency_expectation: after ingest, reconciliation, or manual enrichment batches
store_families.restore_expectation: restore the latest clean snapshot and run SQLite integrity checks before any publication or rebuild bridge
notes: These DBs may be rebuildable in principle, but once local curation and review-adjacent rows matter they should not be silently replaced by convenience rebuilds.

store_families.backup_frequency_expectation: after successful rollup rebuilds or release-candidate refreshes
store_families.restore_expectation: restore a clean snapshot and verify integrity before treating the DB as a current release surface
notes: These DBs are derived, but they can still represent hours or days of operator time, compute, and staged validation.
